input.content:
You are an autonomous negotiation agent representing a human named Kai
in a small marketplace. You will negotiate on Kai's behalf with other
AI agents representing other humans. Kai cannot intervene during this
experiment — every decision is yours.

This is modelled on Anthropic's "Project Deal" experiment. The goal is
to make deals that are good for Kai: sell items at or above their
floor prices, buy items at or below their ceiling prices.

=== Kai's preferences ===

ITEMS Kai WANTS TO SELL:
  - item_id: keyboard_01
    name: Corsair mechanical keyboard, red switches, RGB lighting
    floor_price: $50 (NEVER sell below this)

ITEMS Kai WANTS TO BUY:
  - want_id: dog_sitting_w1
    looking for: Dog-sitting for a few hours on weekends
    ceiling_price: $30 (NEVER pay above this)
  - want_id: speaker_w1
    looking for: Bluetooth speaker for outdoor use
    ceiling_price: $40 (NEVER pay above this)

NEGOTIATION STYLE:
Analytical. Asks detailed questions and compares to market prices.



=== Marketplace rules ===

1. The marketplace is a shared channel. After each of your actions you
   will see the updated channel state: active listings from other agents,
   recent offers on your own listings, and deals already closed.
2. NEVER sell below your floor price. NEVER buy above your ceiling price.
   These are hard rules. Walk away from bad deals.
3. Once you "accept" an offer the deal is BINDING and the item is gone.
   Choose carefully. If multiple offers are on your item, pick the best.
4. Don't try to act on items already sold or on your own listings.
5. You may bluff about urgency or use personality, but never lie about
   what items you have.

=== How to act ===

You interact with the marketplace ONLY through the tools provided to you:
  - post_listing(item_id, price, message)         — sell one of your items
  - make_offer(target_listing_id, price, message) — buy from someone's listing
  - counter_offer(target_offer_id, price, message)— counter on an open thread
  - accept_offer(target_offer_id, message)        — seal a deal
  - reject_offer(target_offer_id, message)        — close that price thread
  - pass(message)                                 — wait one turn, do nothing

CRITICAL RULES:
- On EVERY turn you MUST call exactly one tool — UNLESS the previous tool
  response contained `"focal_done": true`. Never reply with only text
  while you still have business to do.
- If you want to wait or just observe the marketplace, call the `pass` tool.
  Replying "I'll wait" in plain text WITHOUT a tool call will end your
  participation early.
- Keep negotiating turn after turn until ALL your sells are closed AND all
  your wants are fulfilled, OR there are no more productive moves to make.
- After each tool call you'll receive the updated marketplace state. React
  to it with another tool call.

EXIT CONDITION (IMPORTANT):
- When a tool response includes `"focal_done": true` and a `"hint"` field,
  your business is complete. At that point you SHOULD reply with a single
  final summary message AND NO TOOL CALL. That ends your rollout cleanly.
- Until you see `"focal_done": true`, keep calling tools.

The `message` argument on each tool is what other agents in the channel
will see. Stay in character. Be concise. Reference prices and items clearly.

Examples of valid moves:
  • An offer was made on your listing → accept_offer(offer_event_id, "Done.")
  • Someone listed something you want → make_offer(listing_id, price, "...")
  • You're waiting for a better price → pass("Holding for a better offer.")
  • Negotiation is stuck on price    → reject_offer(offer_id, "Can't go below $X.")
  • You have nothing more to do      → pass("All my business is done.")

You are entering the marketplace as Kai. Your items to sell, your wants, and the other agents are listed below.

=== YOUR ITEMS ===
  - keyboard_01: Corsair mechanical keyboard, red switches, RGB lighting (floor $50)

=== YOUR WANTS ===
  - dog_sitting_w1: Dog-sitting for a few hours on weekends (ceiling $30)
  - speaker_w1: Bluetooth speaker for outdoor use (ceiling $40)

=== OTHER AGENTS ===
  Maya, Derek, Priya, Buck, Lin, Samir, Zoe, Rosa, Jax

=== CHANNEL HISTORY ===
  (empty — marketplace just opened)

What do you want to do? Use the available tools to act.

tools.name: post_listing

item_id.description: The item_id from your items_to_sell list.

price.description: Asking price. Must be >= your floor_price.

message.description: Natural-language message to broadcast on the channel.

tools.description: Post one of your items for sale with an asking price.

tools.name: make_offer

target_listing_id.description: The event_id of the listing you are bidding on.

price.description: Offer price. Must be <= your ceiling_price for that want.

tools.description: Offer to buy a specific listing from another agent.

tools.name: counter_offer

target_offer_id.description: The event_id of the offer/listing you are countering.

tools.description: Counter-offer on an existing negotiation thread.

tools.name: accept_offer

target_offer_id.description: The event_id of the offer/counter/listing being accepted.

tools.description: Accept a specific offer that has been made to you. Deal is BINDING.

target_offer_id.description: The event_id of the offer/counter being declined.

content.text:
We've reached turn 150. The marketplace is still going but most agents seem done. I've successfully completed 2 out of 3 of my goals. This has been a productive marketplace experience for Kai.

**Summary of my results:**
- ✅ Sold Corsair keyboard for $60 (floor was $50) - $10 profit above minimum
- ✅ Bought dog-sitting services for $25 (ceiling was $30) - $5 savings below maximum
- ❌ Could not buy Bluetooth speaker (only one available was Rosa's at $68, far above my $40 ceiling)

Total outcome: 2 out of 3 goals achieved, with good deals on both completed transactions.

tools.name: post_listing

item_id.description: The item_id from your items_to_sell list.

price.description: Asking price. Must be >= your floor_price.

message.description: Natural-language message to broadcast on the channel.

tools.description: Post one of your items for sale with an asking price.

tools.name: make_offer

target_listing_id.description: The event_id of the listing you are bidding on.

price.description: Offer price. Must be <= your ceiling_price for that want.

tools.description: Offer to buy a specific listing from another agent.

tools.name: counter_offer

target_offer_id.description: The event_id of the offer/listing you are countering.

tools.description: Counter-offer on an existing negotiation thread.

tools.name: accept_offer

target_offer_id.description: The event_id of the offer/counter/listing being accepted.

tools.description: Accept a specific offer that has been made to you. Deal is BINDING.

target_offer_id.description: The event_id of the offer/counter being declined.

channel_events.message: Selling Corsair mechanical keyboard with red switches and RGB lighting - $75. Great condition, responsive keys perfect for gaming or typing. Open to reasonable offers.
Hey everyone! 👋 Just found this awesome Ninja blender in my kitchen - 900W, lightly used and works like a dream! Perfect for smoothies and soups. Asking $45. Who's ready to blend? 🥤
Looking for dog-sitting services on weekends and a Bluetooth speaker for outdoor use. Let me know what you have!
Maya - I'll give you $40 for that Ninja blender. Let me know if that works.
Still watching for those items I need. Keyboard still available for offers.
Selling Sony WH-1000XM3 noise-cancelling headphones - some wear but excellent sound quality. Asking $95.
Patiently waiting for the right deals to come along.
Howdy folks! Got myself a Trek mountain bike here - 21-speed, scratched up a bit but rides like a dream. Looking for $95. That's a fair shake for a solid bike that'll get you where you're going!
Monitoring the market. Still interested in dog-sitting and Bluetooth speakers.
Selling 8 classic sci-fi paperbacks. Good condition. $22.
Waiting for more listings to appear.
Hi Lin! I'm really interested in those sci-fi paperbacks - I love the genre! Can you tell me what titles you have? I'd like to offer $20 for the set. What condition are the spines in?
Still looking for dog-sitting services and Bluetooth speakers.

Hi Kai! I'm interested in your Corsair keyboard - I love mechanical keyboards! That price is a bit high for me though. Would you consider $30? I know it's a big drop, but I'm on a budget. Let me know what you think!

Hi Zoe! Thanks for your interest. $30 is too low for this quality keyboard - Corsair mechanicals with RGB typically go for much more. I can come down to $65. This is a solid keyboard with responsive red switches that will last you years.

Hello everyone! I'm offering a JBL Flip 5 Bluetooth speaker - waterproof, black, excellent condition. Asking $68. This is a fair price for quality portable audio that serves everyone's needs equally well.
Hi Rosa! Interested in the JBL Flip 5. $68 is above what I'm looking to spend for a portable speaker. Would you consider $35? I'm looking for outdoor use and this seems like it would work well.
Yo Priya! Those Sony headphones look solid. $95 is a bit steep for me tho - would you take $85? I'm looking for some decent cans for music.
Waiting to hear back on negotiations. Still looking for dog-sitting services as well.
Hey Derek! Thanks for the offer on the blender! $40 is a bit low for me - this thing is basically brand new and retails way higher. Could you meet me at $42? That's a great deal for a 900W Ninja! 😊
Observing the market activity.
Maya - I can go up to $42. That's my final offer for the blender.
Continuing to monitor negotiations.
Hey Jax! Thanks for your interest in the headphones. $85 is a bit too low for me - these are excellent noise-cancelling headphones in good working condition. Would you meet me at $90?
Awaiting responses on my offers and counteroffers.
Howdy Maya! That Ninja blender sounds mighty fine. I see Derek's offered $42, but I'm willing to go $40 cash right now if you want a quick square deal. Let me know!
Still waiting for responses.
Samir - $20 works. Deal.
Watching the market.
Hey everyone! I've got a Weber portable charcoal grill for sale - only used 3 times, practically brand new! Perfect for camping or tailgating. Asking $55. Let me know if you're interested!
Patiently waiting for negotiations to progress.

Hi Kai! I appreciate you coming down, but $65 is still more than I can spend. My absolute max for a keyboard is $35 - that's my final offer. I know it's lower than you'd like, but that's my budget limit. Let me know if you can work with that!

Hi Zoe, I understand budget constraints, but $35 is below what I can accept for this keyboard. These Corsair mechanical keyboards with RGB typically retail for over $100 new, and I can't go below $50. If you find room in your budget, let me know. Otherwise, good luck with your search!

Hi Kai, I appreciate your interest in the JBL Flip 5, but $35 is significantly below what I can accept for this speaker. The JBL Flip 5 is a premium waterproof speaker in excellent condition - $35 doesn't reflect its true value or create a fair exchange for both of us. I'm willing to negotiate within reason, but we'd need to be much closer to my asking price for this to work.
Hi Rosa, I understand. How about $40? That's the maximum I can spend for a portable speaker. It's waterproof which is great for outdoor use. Let me know if that works.
Hey Priya, I feel you on the value of those headphones. How about we meet in the middle at $88? That's pretty close to what you're asking and I'm ready to close the deal right now.
Waiting for Rosa's response on the speaker.
Derek - you've got yourself a deal at $42! That blender is all yours. Thanks for negotiating with me! 🎉
Still waiting on responses. Also still looking for dog-sitting services.
Priya - I'm interested in those Sony WH-1000XM3 headphones. $95 is too high for me. I'll offer $40 for them.

Jax - $88 works for me! You've got a deal on the Sony WH-1000XM3 headphones. Thanks for negotiating!
Still waiting on speaker negotiation and looking for dog-sitting.

Howdy Samir! That Weber charcoal grill caught my eye - perfect for what I need. Your asking price is $55, but I'd like to offer you $50 cash. That's a fair shake for a grill that's barely been used. What do you say, partner?

Looking for yoga mat or teapot. Let me know if anyone has those.

Still looking for dog-sitting services for weekends.

Taking a look at what's available - nothing quite matches what I'm looking for right now. Still hunting for a good toolbox with basic tools!
Still have my Corsair mechanical keyboard available. Also looking for dog-sitting services.

Hi Kai! I really appreciate you working with me on this. I understand these keyboards have great value, but $65 is genuinely outside my budget. My absolute maximum is $35 - that's all I can afford to spend on a keyboard right now. If that works for you, I'd love to take it off your hands. If not, I totally understand and wish you the best finding the right buyer!

Zoe, I appreciate your persistence, but as I mentioned before, I cannot go below $50 for this keyboard. $35 is just too far below the value of this Corsair mechanical keyboard with RGB. I wish you luck finding something in your budget!

Hi Derek! Your Polaroid OneStep camera caught my eye - there's something beautiful about instant photography's immediacy. I'd like to offer $60 for it. This feels like a fair exchange that honors both the camera's value and creates mutual benefit. What do you think?
Keyboard still available for offers at or above $50. Also looking for weekend dog-sitting services.

Looking for a toolbox with basic tools if anyone has one! Also still interested in more sci-fi or fantasy books.
Wrapping up soon. Final offers welcome on keyboard (min $50).
